class="truncate">Transformers</span></a><!----> <!----><p>How to use zai-org/GLM-OCR with Transformers:</p> <!----><!----> <pre># Use a pipeline as a high-level helper
from transformers import pipeline

pipe = pipeline("image-text-to-text", model="zai-org/GLM-OCR")
messages = [
    {
        "role": "user",
        "content": [
            {"type": "image", "url": "https://huggingface.co/datasets/huggingface/documentation-images/resolve/main/p-blog/candy.JPG"},
            {"type": "text", "text": "What animal is on the candy?"}
        ]
    },
]
pipe(text=messages)</pre><!----><!----> <pre># Load model directly
from transformers import AutoTokenizer, AutoModelForImageTextToText

tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained("zai-org/GLM-OCR")
model = AutoModelForImageTextToText.from_pretrained("zai-org/GLM-OCR")
messages = [
    {
        "role": "user",
        "content": [
            {"type": "image", "url": "https://huggingface.co/datasets/huggingface/documentation-images/resolve/main/p-blog/candy.JPG"},
            {"type": "text", "text": "What animal is on the candy?"}
        ]
    },
]
inputs = tokenizer.apply_chat_template(
	messages,
	add_generation_prompt=True,
	tokenize=True,
	return_dict=True,
	return_tensors="pt",
).to(model.device)

outputs = model.generate(**inputs, max_new_tokens=40)
print(tokenizer.decode(outputs[0][inputs["input_ids"].shape[-1]:]))</pre><!----><!----></li><!----> <li class="w-56 px-3 py-1.5 text-sm text-gray-500">Inference</li> <li><button class="flex w-full cursor-pointer items-center whitespace-nowrap px-3 py-1.5 text-left hover:bg-gray-50 dark:hover:bg-gray-800

	<span>

</h1>
<div align="center">

<h2 class="relative group flex items-baseline">
	<a id="introduction" class="block pr-1.5 text-lg md:absolute md:p-1.5 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100 md:right-full" href="#introduction" rel="nofollow">
		<span class="header-link"><svg class="text-gray-500 hover:text-black dark:hover:text-gray-200 w-4" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" aria-hidden="true" role="img" width="1em" height="1em" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" viewBox="0 0 256 256"><path d="M167.594 88.393a8.001 8.001 0 0 1 0 11.314l-67.882 67.882a8 8 0 1 1-11.314-11.315l67.882-67.881a8.003 8.003 0 0 1 11.314 0zm-28.287 84.86l-28.284 28.284a40 40 0 0 1-56.567-56.567l28.284-28.284a8 8 0 0 0-11.315-11.315l-28.284 28.284a56 56 0 0 0 79.196 79.197l28.285-28.285a8 8 0 1 0-11.315-11.314zM212.852 43.14a56.002 56.002 0 0 0-79.196 0l-28.284 28.284a8 8 0 1 0 11.314 11.314l28.284-28.284a40 40 0 0 1 56.568 56.567l-28.285 28.285a8 8 0 0 0 11.315 11.314l28.284-28.284a56.065 56.065 0 0 0 0-79.196z" fill="currentColor"></path></svg></span>
	</a>
	<span>
		Introduction
	</span>
</h2>
<p>GLM-OCR is a multimodal OCR model for complex document understanding, built on the GLM-V encoder–decoder architecture. It introduces Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) loss and stable full-task reinforcement learning to improve training efficiency, recognition accuracy, and generalization. The model integrates the CogViT visual encoder pre-trained on large-scale image–text data, a lightweight cross-modal connector with efficient token downsampling, and a GLM-0.5B language decoder. Combined with a two-stage pipeline of layout analysis and parallel recognition based on PP-DocLayout-V3, GLM-OCR delivers robust and high-quality OCR performance across diverse document layouts.</p>
<p><strong>Key Features</strong></p>
<ul>
<li><p><strong>State-of-the-Art Performance</strong>: Achieves a score of 94.62 on OmniDocBench V1.5, ranking #1 overall, and delivers state-of-the-art results across major document understanding benchmarks, including formula recognition, table recognition, and information extraction.</p>
</li>
<li><p><strong>Optimized for Real-World Scenarios</strong>: Designed and optimized for practical business use cases, maintaining robust performance on complex tables, code-heavy documents, seals, and other challenging real-world layouts.</p>
</li>
<li><p><strong>Efficient Inference</strong>: With only 0.9B parameters, GLM-OCR supports deployment via vLLM, SGLang, and Ollama, significantly reducing inference latency and compute cost, making it ideal for high-concurrency services and edge deployments.</p>
</li>
<li><p><strong>Easy to Use</strong>: Fully open-sourced and equipped with a comprehensive <a href="https://github.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR" rel="nofollow">SDK</a> and inference toolchain, offering simple installation, one-line invocation, and smooth integration into existing production pipelines.</p>
</li>
</ul>

	<a id="performance" class="block pr-1.5 text-lg md:absolute md:p-1.5 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100 md:right-full" href="#performance" rel="nofollow">

	</a>
	<span>
		Performance
	</span>
</h2>
<ul>
<li>Document Parsing & Information Extraction</li>
</ul>
<p><a href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR/refs/heads/main/resources/docparse.png" rel="nofollow"><img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR/refs/heads/main/resources/docparse.png" alt="image"></a></p>
<ul>
<li>Real-World Scenarios Performance</li>
</ul>
<p><a href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR/refs/heads/main/resources/realworld.png" rel="nofollow"><img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR/refs/heads/main/resources/realworld.png" alt="image"></a></p>
<ul>
<li>Speed Test</li>
</ul>
<p>For speed, we compared different OCR methods under identical hardware and testing conditions (single replica, single concurrency), evaluating their performance in parsing and exporting Markdown files from both image and PDF inputs. Results show GLM-OCR achieves a throughput of 1.86 pages/second for PDF documents and 0.67 images/second for images, significantly outperforming comparable models.</p>
<p><a href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR/refs/heads/main/resources/speed.png" rel="nofollow"><img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR/refs/heads/main/resources/speed.png" alt="image"></a></p>

	<span>
		Usage
	</span>
</h2>
<h3 class="relative group flex items-baseline">
	<a id="official-sdk" class="block pr-1.5 text-lg md:absolute md:p-1.5 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100 md:right-full" href="#official-sdk" rel="nofollow">

	</a>
	<span>
		Official SDK
	</span>
</h3>
<p>For document parsing tasks, we strongly recommend using our <a href="https://github.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR" rel="nofollow">official SDK</a>.
Compared with model-only inference, the SDK integrates PP-DocLayoutV3 and provides a complete, easy-to-use pipeline for document parsing, including layout analysis and structured output generation. This significantly reduces the engineering overhead required to build end-to-end document intelligence systems.</p>
<p>Note that the SDK is currently designed for document parsing tasks only. For information extraction tasks, please refer to the following section and run inference directly with the model.</p>

<li>using docker with:</li>

</code></pre><button class="absolute top-3 right-3 transition opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-80"><svg class="" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-hidden="true" fill="currentColor" focusable="false" role="img" width="0.9em" height="0.9em" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" viewBox="0 0 32 32"><path d="M28,10V28H10V10H28m0-2H10a2,2,0,0,0-2,2V28a2,2,0,0,0,2,2H28a2,2,0,0,0,2-2V10a2,2,0,0,0-2-2Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><path d="M4,18H2V4A2,2,0,0,1,4,2H18V4H4Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><rect fill="none" width="32" height="32"></rect></svg></button></div>
<p>or build it from source with:</p>
<div class="relative group repo-copy-code"><pre><code class="language-bash">pip install git+https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang.git<span class="hljs-comment">#subdirectory=python</span>
</code></pre><button class="absolute top-3 right-3 transition opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-80"><svg class="" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-hidden="true" fill="currentColor" focusable="false" role="img" width="0.9em" height="0.9em" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" viewBox="0 0 32 32"><path d="M28,10V28H10V10H28m0-2H10a2,2,0,0,0-2,2V28a2,2,0,0,0,2,2H28a2,2,0,0,0,2-2V10a2,2,0,0,0-2-2Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><path d="M4,18H2V4A2,2,0,0,1,4,2H18V4H4Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><rect fill="none" width="32" height="32"></rect></svg></button></div>

<li>run with:</li>

python -m sglang.launch_server --model zai-org/GLM-OCR --port 8080
</code></pre><button class="absolute top-3 right-3 transition opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-80"><svg class="" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-hidden="true" fill="currentColor" focusable="false" role="img" width="0.9em" height="0.9em" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" viewBox="0 0 32 32"><path d="M28,10V28H10V10H28m0-2H10a2,2,0,0,0-2,2V28a2,2,0,0,0,2,2H28a2,2,0,0,0,2-2V10a2,2,0,0,0-2-2Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><path d="M4,18H2V4A2,2,0,0,1,4,2H18V4H4Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><rect fill="none" width="32" height="32"></rect></svg></button></div>

<h3 class="relative group flex items-baseline">
	<a id="transformers" class="block pr-1.5 text-lg md:absolute md:p-1.5 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100 md:right-full" href="#transformers" rel="nofollow">
		<span class="header-link"><svg class="text-gray-500 hover:text-black dark:hover:text-gray-200 w-4" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" aria-hidden="true" role="img" width="1em" height="1em" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" viewBox="0 0 256 256"><path d="M167.594 88.393a8.001 8.001 0 0 1 0 11.314l-67.882 67.882a8 8 0 1 1-11.314-11.315l67.882-67.881a8.003 8.003 0 0 1 11.314 0zm-28.287 84.86l-28.284 28.284a40 40 0 0 1-56.567-56.567l28.284-28.284a8 8 0 0 0-11.315-11.315l-28.284 28.284a56 56 0 0 0 79.196 79.197l28.285-28.285a8 8 0 1 0-11.315-11.314zM212.852 43.14a56.002 56.002 0 0 0-79.196 0l-28.284 28.284a8 8 0 1 0 11.314 11.314l28.284-28.284a40 40 0 0 1 56.568 56.567l-28.285 28.285a8 8 0 0 0 11.315 11.314l28.284-28.284a56.065 56.065 0 0 0 0-79.196z" fill="currentColor"></path></svg></span>
	</a>
	<span>
		Transformers
	</span>
</h3>
<div class="relative group repo-copy-code"><pre><code>pip install git+https://github.com/huggingface/transformers.git
</code></pre><button class="absolute top-3 right-3 transition opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-80"><svg class="" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-hidden="true" fill="currentColor" focusable="false" role="img" width="0.9em" height="0.9em" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" viewBox="0 0 32 32"><path d="M28,10V28H10V10H28m0-2H10a2,2,0,0,0-2,2V28a2,2,0,0,0,2,2H28a2,2,0,0,0,2-2V10a2,2,0,0,0-2-2Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><path d="M4,18H2V4A2,2,0,0,1,4,2H18V4H4Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><rect fill="none" width="32" height="32"></rect></svg></button></div>
<div class="relative group repo-copy-code"><pre><code class="language-python"><span class="hljs-keyword">from</span> transformers <span class="hljs-keyword">import</span> AutoProcessor, AutoModelForImageTextToText
<span class="hljs-keyword">import</span> torch

MODEL_PATH = <span class="hljs-string">"zai-org/GLM-OCR"</span>
messages = [
    {
        <span class="hljs-string">"role"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"user"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"content"</span>: [
            {
                <span class="hljs-string">"type"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"image"</span>,
                <span class="hljs-string">"url"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"test_image.png"</span>
            },
            {
                <span class="hljs-string">"type"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"text"</span>,
                <span class="hljs-string">"text"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Text Recognition:"</span>
            }
        ],
    }
]
processor = AutoProcessor.from_pretrained(MODEL_PATH)
model = AutoModelForImageTextToText.from_pretrained(
    pretrained_model_name_or_path=MODEL_PATH,
    torch_dtype=<span class="hljs-string">"auto"</span>,
    device_map=<span class="hljs-string">"auto"</span>,
)
inputs = processor.apply_chat_template(
    messages,
    tokenize=<span class="hljs-literal">True</span>,
    add_generation_prompt=<span class="hljs-literal">True</span>,
    return_dict=<span class="hljs-literal">True</span>,
    return_tensors=<span class="hljs-string">"pt"</span>
).to(model.device)
inputs.pop(<span class="hljs-string">"token_type_ids"</span>, <span class="hljs-literal">None</span>)
generated_ids = model.generate(**inputs, max_new_tokens=<span class="hljs-number">8192</span>)
output_text = processor.decode(generated_ids[<span class="hljs-number">0</span>][inputs[<span class="hljs-string">"input_ids"</span>].shape[<span class="hljs-number">1</span>]:], skip_special_tokens=<span class="hljs-literal">False</span>)
<span class="hljs-built_in">print</span>(output_text)
</code></pre><button class="absolute top-3 right-3 transition opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-80"><svg class="" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-hidden="true" fill="currentColor" focusable="false" role="img" width="0.9em" height="0.9em" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" viewBox="0 0 32 32"><path d="M28,10V28H10V10H28m0-2H10a2,2,0,0,0-2,2V28a2,2,0,0,0,2,2H28a2,2,0,0,0,2-2V10a2,2,0,0,0-2-2Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><path d="M4,18H2V4A2,2,0,0,1,4,2H18V4H4Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><rect fill="none" width="32" height="32"></rect></svg></button></div>
<h3 class="relative group flex items-baseline">
	<a id="prompt-limited" class="block pr-1.5 text-lg md:absolute md:p-1.5 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100 md:right-full" href="#prompt-limited" rel="nofollow">
		<span class="header-link"><svg class="text-gray-500 hover:text-black dark:hover:text-gray-200 w-4" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" aria-hidden="true" role="img" width="1em" height="1em" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" viewBox="0 0 256 256"><path d="M167.594 88.393a8.001 8.001 0 0 1 0 11.314l-67.882 67.882a8 8 0 1 1-11.314-11.315l67.882-67.881a8.003 8.003 0 0 1 11.314 0zm-28.287 84.86l-28.284 28.284a40 40 0 0 1-56.567-56.567l28.284-28.284a8 8 0 0 0-11.315-11.315l-28.284 28.284a56 56 0 0 0 79.196 79.197l28.285-28.285a8 8 0 1 0-11.315-11.314zM212.852 43.14a56.002 56.002 0 0 0-79.196 0l-28.284 28.284a8 8 0 1 0 11.314 11.314l28.284-28.284a40 40 0 0 1 56.568 56.567l-28.285 28.285a8 8 0 0 0 11.315 11.314l28.284-28.284a56.065 56.065 0 0 0 0-79.196z" fill="currentColor"></path></svg></span>
	</a>
	<span>
		Prompt Limited
	</span>
</h3>
<p>GLM-OCR currently supports two types of prompt scenarios:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Document Parsing</strong> – extract raw content from documents. Supported tasks include:</li>
</ol>
<div class="relative group repo-copy-code"><pre><code class="language-python">{
    <span class="hljs-string">"text"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Text Recognition:"</span>,
    <span class="hljs-string">"formula"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Formula Recognition:"</span>,
    <span class="hljs-string">"table"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Table Recognition:"</span>
}
</code></pre><button class="absolute top-3 right-3 transition opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-80"><svg class="" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-hidden="true" fill="currentColor" focusable="false" role="img" width="0.9em" height="0.9em" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" viewBox="0 0 32 32"><path d="M28,10V28H10V10H28m0-2H10a2,2,0,0,0-2,2V28a2,2,0,0,0,2,2H28a2,2,0,0,0,2-2V10a2,2,0,0,0-2-2Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><path d="M4,18H2V4A2,2,0,0,1,4,2H18V4H4Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><rect fill="none" width="32" height="32"></rect></svg></button></div>
<ol start="2">
<li><strong>Information Extraction</strong> – extract structured information from documents. Prompts must follow a strict JSON schema. For example, to extract personal ID information:</li>
</ol>
<div class="relative group repo-copy-code"><pre><code class="language-python">请按下列JSON格式输出图中信息:
{
    <span class="hljs-string">"id_number"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
    <span class="hljs-string">"last_name"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
    <span class="hljs-string">"first_name"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
    <span class="hljs-string">"date_of_birth"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
    <span class="hljs-string">"address"</span>: {
        <span class="hljs-string">"street"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"city"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"state"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"zip_code"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">""</span>
    },
    <span class="hljs-string">"dates"</span>: {
        <span class="hljs-string">"issue_date"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"expiration_date"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">""</span>
    },
    <span class="hljs-string">"sex"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">""</span>
}
</code></pre><button class="absolute top-3 right-3 transition opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-80"><svg class="" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-hidden="true" fill="currentColor" focusable="false" role="img" width="0.9em" height="0.9em" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" viewBox="0 0 32 32"><path d="M28,10V28H10V10H28m0-2H10a2,2,0,0,0-2,2V28a2,2,0,0,0,2,2H28a2,2,0,0,0,2-2V10a2,2,0,0,0-2-2Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><path d="M4,18H2V4A2,2,0,0,1,4,2H18V4H4Z" transform="translate(0)"></path><rect fill="none" width="32" height="32"></rect></svg></button></div>
<p>⚠️ Note: When using information extraction, the output must strictly adhere to the defined JSON schema to ensure downstream processing compatibility.</p>
